subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Clear narrative with introduction, body and conclusion, supported by headings and numbered equations
Logical development of the model with step-by-step explanations
Mostly relevant discussion focused on the drug-concentration modelling
weaknesses: Abrupt transitions (e.g., sudden reliance on ChatGPT suggestion) interrupt flow
Algebraic slips and inconsistent TOC formatting detract from coherence
Verbose repetitions (RÂ² explanations, variable definitions) reduce conciseness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three Presentation strands are placed in band 3: the work is coherent with clear logical development and well organized, but not exceptional due to abrupt transitions, minor errors and verbosity. It meets the âwell organizedâ and ârelevant and mostly conciseâ descriptors, warranting 3/4.

strengths: Consistent use of mathematical notation for functions, logarithms, exponents and derivatives
Effective use of tables, scatter and semi-log plots, trend-lines and an annotated composite-model diagram
Clear explanations linking regression, composite functions and geometric sequences
weaknesses: Inconsistent notation (e.g., ln t vs lx, mismatched parentheses)
Minor confusion from rounding inconsistencies and an incorrect constant (2.3411 vs 2.4311)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical Communication is in band 3 across all strands: mostly appropriate notation, effective but not sophisticated representations, and clear but occasionally inconsistent explanations. These meet the âmostly appropriateâ¦â, âsome appropriateâ¦â and âmostly clearâ¦â descriptors, justifying 3/4.

strengths: Personal motivation clearly drives the choice of problem (brotherâs treatment)
Independent initiative shown by shifting the model by 2 hours to reflect physiology
Creative combination of exponential decay with geometric modelling
weaknesses: Reliance on an external 5 % rule rather than original deduction
Exploration follows standard HL techniques, limiting originality to significant rather than outstanding
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Personal Engagement strands fall in band 2: there is clear evidence of independent thinking, a personal approach and creativity, but not at an outstanding level. This aligns with the âclear personal approachâ and âsignificant creativityâ descriptors, yielding 2/3.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on data limitations, simplifications and possible extensions
Critical analysis in comparing RÂ² values and discussing oversimplification
Clear connection between reflection and the explorationâs aim of patient understanding
weaknesses: Reflection remains largely qualitative without quantitative error or sensitivity analysis
Depth of critique is limitedâno exploration of alternative modelling frameworks or parameter sensitivity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All Reflection strands are in band 2: the student offers meaningful reflection, basic critical analysis and a clear connection to understanding, but does not demonstrate the substantial, deep critique required for band 3. Thus 2/3 is appropriate.

strengths: Purposeful use of HL-level mathematics (exponential decay, composite functions, differentiation)
Thorough understanding of regression techniques and domain handling (ln(0) point removal)
Critical evaluation of models and sound transformation into a geometric series
weaknesses: Occasional algebraic errors (e^{2.3411} vs e^{2.4311}) propagate minor inaccuracies
Justification for the 5 % rule and time-shift lacks quantitative support
Conceptual gaps in the rationale for excluding initial data points
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Use of Mathematics is placed in band 4: relevant course-level mathematics is used effectively and with minor errors. Though understanding and accuracy show occasional lapses (band 3), the overall work meets the ârelevant and effectiveâ and âcorrect with minor errorsâ descriptors, justifying 4/6.

strandSelections.reasoning: Selecting, linearising and subsequently rejecting one model, then constructing a composite and series approach, shows notable independent thinking beyond replicating a textbook technique, though some steps (5 % rule) rely on external suggestion rather than original deduction.

The student shows good conceptual understanding of exponential decay, composite functions and geometric sums, explaining the logic behind each manipulation. Occasional conceptual gaps (why shift by exactly two hours, why 5 % defines steady state) mean understanding is strong in parts but not thorough throughout.

Calculations are broadly correct and internally consistent, but there are noticeable slips: mis-typing of 2.4311 as 2.3411, unexplained constant rounding, and reliance on an arbitrary 5 % rule. These lead to minor inaccuracies; therefore accuracy is âmostly correctâ, not fully correct.

Most discussion is relevant to modelling the drug concentration, but the narrative is verbose: multiple explanations of RÂ², repeated definitions of variables and extensive quotations about geometric sequences add length without insight. Hence the work is ârelevant and mostly conciseâ, not consistently concise.

Notation for functions, logarithms, exponents and derivatives is generally correct and used throughout. Minor inconsistencies (e.g., switching between ln t and lx, mismatched parentheses, occasional bold variables) show that the language is not perfectly accurate, but it remains mostly appropriate.

Explanations are generally clear and consistent; variables are defined when first used and reasoning is stated. Occasional lapses (incorrect constant 2.3411 vs 2.4311, inconsistent rounding policy) create minor confusion, preventing a fully consistent communication.

Exponential decay, transformation to a geometric series, use of composite functions and differentiation are all at HL course level and used purposefully to obtain the result; thus the mathematics is relevant and used effectively. The work does not reach a level of rigour (e.g., differential equations, non-linear regression) that would be considered âsophisticatedâ.

The student presents tables of raw data, scatter plots, semi-log plots, trend-lines, fitted exponential curves and an annotated diagram of the cumulative model. These representations are chosen effectively to justify model selection, but there is no higher-level comparison (e.g., residual plot) that would lift it to a âsophisticatedâ use.

The student reflects on data limitations, personal simplifications and possible extensions, demonstrating meaningful reflection. The discussion remains fairly general, without probing alternative modelling frameworks or quantitative error analysis that would constitute âsubstantialâ reflection.

Turning a pharmacokinetic problem into an exponentialâgeometric model and shifting the function to align with physiological behaviour shows creativity and initiative. The mathematics, however, stays within standard HL techniques, so the evidence is significant rather than outstanding.

The investigation follows a clear narrative: introduction of context, construction of a single-dose model, extension to cumulative doses, derivation, evaluation and conclusion. Each mathematical step is explained in sequence, so the reader can follow how decisions lead to the next section. Some transitions (e.g., the sudden reliance on ChatGPT for a 5 % rate) are abrupt and a few algebraic slips break the flow, preventing the work from being judged âhighlyâ coherent.

Critical analysis is shown when the student compares RÂ² values, recognises negative predictions, and discusses oversimplification. Nonetheless, deeper critique of parameter estimation, residuals or sensitivity is absent, keeping the work in the âmeaningfulâ rather than âdeepâ band.

Reflection clearly links back to the stated aim of helping patients anticipate steady state and mentions how mathematical choices affect understanding. The link enhances understanding but is not extensively developed, placing it in band 2.

A table of contents, headings, sub-headings and numbered equations give the exploration a recognisable introductionâbodyâevaluationâconclusion structure. However, some sections are out of order (evaluation placed inside differentiation section) and a few long blocks of copied spreadsheet output disturb the otherwise neat layout, so the organisation is âwell organisedâ rather than âexceptionallyâ so.

The exploration is clearly motivated by the studentâs brotherâs situation and personal curiosity; this personal context drives the choice of problem and the modelling path, satisfying a âclear personal approachâ but not an âoutstandingâ one.

annotations.comment: Proposed improvements are logical but remain qualitative. Including a plan for quantitative sensitivity analysis or expanded data acquisition would deepen the reflection.
The conclusion restates objectives extensively, leading to verbosity. Focus on summarizing key findings succinctly and referencing how the result meets the aim.
References vary in style and formatting. Adhering to a consistent citation style (e.g., APA or MLA) will enhance presentation quality.

The Table of Contents includes a stray entry â. 9â and inconsistent formatting, which disrupts navigation and clarity. Consider revising the TOC entries to align page numbers with section titles and remove placeholders.
The student effectively integrates personal motivation by linking the exploration to their brotherâs treatment, demonstrating clear personal engagement that drives the investigation.
The objectives and significance are well outlined, but the discussion is verbose. Consider condensing repeated points about mental health to enhance conciseness and focus.
The raw data table is presented clearly with appropriate units, facilitating transparent observation of the drug concentration over time.
While Graph 1 illustrates the data trend, the axis labels and tick marks in the embedded image are small. Enlarging or annotating axes will improve readability.
Omitting the first 2 hours from modeling is reasonable, but lacks quantitative justification. Provide a brief error analysis or residual comparison to support this exclusion.
Graph 2 effectively shows data post-2 hours, but adding a residual plot would strengthen model validation by revealing any systematic deviations.
The student clearly explains the rationale for linearizing data and comparing RÂ² values, demonstrating sound understanding of regression techniques.
Identifying and removing the ln(0) data point demonstrates careful handling of domain restrictions in logarithmic transformations.
The pasted raw Excel trendline output is cluttered and difficult to interpret. Reformat or recreate the graphs directly in this document for clarity.
Inconsistent notation appears (e.g., switching between ln t and lx, stray symbols like â2). Ensure mathematical expressions are consistently formatted and correctly rendered.
Demonstrating critical evaluation by rejecting the highestâRÂ² model due to negative predictions shows mature understanding of model applicability.
The selected exponential model underpredicts early concentrations. Consider quantifying this deviation (e.g., percent error) to justify its acceptability over alternative models.
Shifting the model by 2 hours to align with the peak concentration demonstrates independent initiative to improve model realism.
The rationale for creating a composite function to reparameterize time in 6-hour units is presented clearly, aiding comprehension of the cumulative model.
Notation for C(t(n)) and C(n) could be unified to avoid confusion between functions of t and n; define symbols consistently when introducing composite functions.
The manual calculation of multiple ingestion terms is correct but repetitive. Introducing the geometric series formula earlier could streamline the presentation.
Linking the composite model to a geometric sequence demonstrates strong mathematical communication by connecting theory and application.
Transforming the composite model into standard geometric series form and deriving r and Uâ reflects thorough understanding of sequence manipulation.
Explaining the adjustment from standard series starting index to a 0th term enhances clarity of the exponent modification in the final model.
An incorrect constant e^{2.3411} appears here instead of e^{2.4311}, which propagates an algebraic error. Verify and correct this coefficient.
After solving for n, specify that this term count multiplies by 6 hours and include the +2 hours offset directly with units to make the final time interpretation explicit.
The student critically reflects on data limitations, individual variability, and oversimplification, demonstrating meaningful reflection on model constraints.
